title: Aloo Paratha

subs.name: a squeeze of lemon juice mixed into the filling

steps.text: Mix atta with a pinch of salt in a bowl. Add water gradually and knead into a soft, smooth dough. Cover with a damp cloth and rest for 20 minutes.

In a bowl combine the mashed potatoes, green chilli, ginger, coriander leaves, cumin, amchur, red chilli powder, garam masala and salt. Mix well. The filling should hold its shape when pinched — if it is wet, the paratha will tear.

Divide the dough into 8 equal portions. Roll one portion into a small circle about 10 cm across. Place 2 tablespoons of filling in the centre and gather the edges upward, pinching tightly to seal. Flatten the stuffed ball gently.
Dust the sealed ball and roll it carefully into a round about 18 cm wide. Work gently and evenly so the filling stays inside. If a small tear appears, press the dough together and continue rolling.

Heat a tawa on medium-high. Place the paratha on the dry tawa and cook for 90 seconds until the underside looks dry with small brown spots. Flip, drizzle half a teaspoon of ghee around the edges, and cook another 60 seconds.

Flip once more, add another small drizzle of ghee, and press gently with a spatula to encourage even browning. The paratha is done when both sides show deep golden patches and the edges are slightly crisp. Serve immediately with curd and pickle.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: amchur → a squeeze of lemon juice mixed into the filling; ghee → butter or neutral oil (ghee gives the best flavour); green-chilli → black pepper for less heat
